old_claim.note: Yokes & Harnesses provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No separate yoke edge remains because the source defines the innovation as a seed funnel on a traction plow, while animal_drawn_plows already represents the complete pulling arrangement.

source_shape.source_locator: Page 178 locates the innovation in the funnel behind the share and describes the field team without identifying a novel yoke or harness as part of the seeder mechanism.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The feature separating the seeder-plow from an ordinary traction plow is controlled seed delivery, not a distinct harness design.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A direct harness edge would model generic traction twice and would not explain the seed-metering capability that now defines the node.

ontology_before: Generic traction equipment was attached directly to a node ambiguously described as heavy draft plowing.
ontology_after: The scoped seeder innovation has one immediate machine prerequisite, with harnessing represented within that upstream traction-plow system.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: heavy_draft_plowing no longer directly depends on yokes_and_harnesses.
would_reject_if: A source demonstrated that the Early Dynastic seeder-plow required a distinct yoke technology not already represented by animal_drawn_plows.
short_reason: The seed funnel, not a separate harness, is the scoped implement innovation.
